report_name: 'The Gentlemen' Ransomware Group Claims Attack on Czech Energy Firm Energon

threat_actors.name: The Gentlemen
threat_actors.description: A financially motivated cybercriminal group operating a ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) model. The group claimed responsibility for the attack on Energon, employing a double extortion strategy involving data encryption and threatening to leak exfiltrated data.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Gentlemen ransomware was deployed to encrypt files on Energon's systems, disrupting operations as part of a double extortion scheme.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Sensitive data was successfully exfiltrated from Energon's corporate IT environment before encryption to be used as leverage in a double extortion scheme. The hunting hints suggest cloud storage services are a likely destination.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware operators associated with The Gentlemen likely delete volume shadow copies using 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows' to prevent easy system recovery from local backups.

malware.name: The Gentlemen Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by The Gentlemen group. It encrypts victim files and is associated with a double extortion scheme where stolen data is threatened to be leaked.

identities.name: Energon
identities.description: A prominent energy services and sustainable technology company based in the Czech Republic, targeted by The Gentlemen ransomware group in a double extortion attack.

infrastructure.name: The Gentlemen Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web data leak site used by The Gentlemen group to list victims and threaten the publication of stolen data if a ransom is not paid.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 7c5b9f3a-1e8d-4f6a-a2b1-9c8d7e6f5a4b
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/thegentlemen-ransomware-strikes-energon-in-czech-republic/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a command-line pattern for deleting volume shadow copies, a common ransomware behavior mentioned as a hunting hint for The Gentlemen group's activity.

indicators.name: The Gentlemen Encrypted File Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: The Gentlemen Encrypted File Detection
id: 3d1e0a9c-8b7a-4f5e-9d6c-1b2a3f4e5d6c
status: experimental
description: Detects files created with the '.thegentlemen' extension, a possible indicator of encryption by The Gentlemen ransomware.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/thegentlemen-ransomware-strikes-energon-in-czech-republic/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.thegentlemen'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of files with a possible '.thegentlemen' extension, as suggested by hunting hints for The Gentlemen ransomware.

indicators.name: Potential Data Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Data Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
id: 8e4d2c1b-9a0f-4e3d-8b2a-1c9d8e7f6a5b
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to common cloud storage services that could be used for data exfiltration prior to a ransomware attack.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/thegentlemen-ransomware-strikes-energon-in-czech-republic/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
tags:
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|endswith:
      - 'mega.nz'
      - 'dropbox.com'
      - 'mega.io'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to popular cloud storage services, which could indicate data exfiltration as suggested in the hunting hints for The Gentlemen group.

relationships.source_name: Potential Data Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
